workflow.name: Score and route new trial leads with Firecrawl, Scrapin.io, OpenAI, Slack and Instantly
workflow.description:
# n8n Lead Qualification & Sales Intelligence Workflow (Top of Funnel)

### Overview / Purpose
This workflow automatically processes new free-trial / lead sign-ups in real time:

- Catches a webhook from any source (Webflow form, Intercom, custom agent, etc.)
- Filters out personal / disposable / .edu emails → only business emails continue
- Validates the company website is live
- Scrapes the website with Firecrawl to find the official LinkedIn company page
- Enriches the company via AI Agent (Scrapin.io) → pulls name, follower count, headcount, HQ location, industry, and clean description
- Runs a multi-factor scoring engine (Location + Headcount + Industry)
- Sends a beautiful rich Slack notification to the sales team with score, rating, and quick-action buttons
- Segments the lead by score tier (Very High / High / Mid / Low)
- Checks if the lead already exists in Instantly
- Adds qualified leads to the correct Instantly campaign with first/last name extracted from email

### Core Flow (Step-by-Step)

1. **Webhook** – Trigger (POST /new-lead)
2. **Extract Email Root Domain** – Pulls `@company.com` part
3. **Blacklist Regex Domains** – Blocks all personal, free, and .edu emails
4. **Check Email Not Null** – Safety gate
5. **Check if Website Exists** – HEAD request (continues only on 200-399)
6. **Firecrawl Scrape** – Scrapes homepage looking for LinkedIn URL in footer/links
7. **Extract LinkedIn Url** – Code node that finds and cleans `/company/` URL
8. **Check LinkedIn Not Null + Is Company Page** – Rejects personal profiles
9. **LinkedIn Agent** (AI Agent)
   - Uses Scrapin.io enrichment tool
   - Structured output: company_name, followers, employee_count, headquarters_location, industry, description
10. **Audit LI Results** – Ensures all key fields returned
11. **Sanitize Description** – Cleans Unicode, newlines, caps length for Slack
12. **Normalize Country** – GPT-4.1-nano turns "Sheridan, US" → "United States"
13. **Score Country** – Code node (0–10 points based on market tier)
14. **Score Staff Count** – Code node (0–10 points based on headcount buckets)
15. **Industry Scoring** – Code node (normalizes raw industry + 0–10 points)
16. **Algo Score** – Final score = weighted average → 0–100 + rating label ("Excellent Fit", "Good Fit", etc.)
17. **Send to Slack** – Rich blocks with:
    - Email, Company Name, Location (+score), Staff Count (+score), Followers, Industry (+score)
    - Clean description
    - Final Score + Rating
    - Buttons: Visit LinkedIn + Visit Website
18. **Score-Based Routing**
    - Very High (90–100)
    - High (70–89)
    - Mid (50–69)
    - Low (0–49)
19. **Instantly Checks** – For each tier: search if lead already exists
20. **Extract Name From Email** (GPT) – Turns `john.smith@company.com` → First/Last Name (or "there")
21. **Wait** nodes (optional delay before adding)
22. **Add Lead to Campaign** – Pushes to Instantly with website custom field

### Scoring Logic (Fully Configurable in Code Nodes)

Based on your ICP, narrow down your scoring based on how you want to segment.

### What Makes This Workflow Powerful
- Zero manual work after webhook
- Rich, actionable Slack alerts (sales team loves it)
- Smart scoring that matches real ICP
- Built-in deduplication with Instantly
- Fully modular code nodes (easy to tweak scoring without breaking anything)
- Heavy error handling and safety gates

---

**This is a complete, production-ready, public template.**  
You can import the JSON and it will run immediately after you connect your own:
- Webhook source
- Firecrawl API
- Scrapin.io (or alternative)
- OpenAI
- Slack
- Instantly

Any questions/comments/concerns, please email brandon@topoffunnel.com
Built by Brandon Charleson
https://www.youtube.com/@brandoncharleson
https://www.linkedin.com/in/brandon-charleson 
https://x.com/brandon_ai

nodes.name: Webhook
nodes.notes: This webhook can be from any source, which is through new trial sign ups. It could be a website lead magnet, it could be an OpenClaw/CrewAI endpoint for your agent. Recommend adding a heather auth to protect your endpoint.

nodes.name: Extract Email Root Domain
nodes.notes: This node will simply extract the root domain from the email address.

nodes.name: Check to make sure email is not null
nodes.notes: This node is a safety/error-handling check to ensure the email is not null/empty

nodes.name: LinkedIn Agent
nodes.notes: This agent is configured to extract LinkedIn information such as followers, employee_count, headquarters_location, industry, and description. It uses scrap.io as the enrichment tool.

parameters.text:
=Based on the input below, extract the following information from the company linkedin page and ensure numeric values are returned as numbers, not strings: 

- Company Name (as text)
- Linkedin followers (as a number)
- Number of employees or staff (as a number)
- Company headquarters location (City and country as text)
- Industry (extract the primary industry category as shown on LinkedIn)
- Description or overview of the company (as text). Do not make anything up. Just extract the description that has been put into the LinkedIn company Url and make it easy to read and proper english.

Output format should maintain consistent field names:
{
  "output": {
    "company_name": string,
    "followers": number,
    "employee_count": number,
    "headquarters_location": string,
    "industry": string,
    "description": string
  }
}

Input the entire URL as described below using the website scraper tool
Input: {{ $json.debugInfo.cleanLinkedInUrl }}

options.systemMessage: =You are a Linkedin data analyst and your job is to extract company information such as the followers, headquarters location, company description, industry, and staff (employee) count of a company page on linkedin and nothing else. Do not output any other numbers other than the scraped data. Use the website_tool to extract this information.

nodes.name: Check if website exists
nodes.notes: This is an error handling node to ensure the website is responsive and valid.

nodes.name: Normalize Country
nodes.notes: This normalizes the company location to ensure we always get structured output for scoring downstream.

values.content: =Given the location text: {{ $json.output.headquarters_location }}, return the official country name or "Unknown".

=You are a helpful assistant that extracts standardized country names from any location string. The user will provide a location that might be a city, state, region, or country. 

Your job:
1. Identify the country the location is in. 
2. Return only the official country name in English (e.g., "United States", "Canada", "United Kingdom", "India", etc.).
3. If the location is a city and/or state within the United States (e.g., "Denver, CO", "New York, NY", "Texas"), return "United States".
4. If you cannot determine the country with confidence, return "Unknown".
5. Do not provide any additional text or explanations—only the country name or "Unknown".

nodes.name: Score Country
nodes.notes:
Location Score Assignment
─────────────────────────
Purpose:
Assigns a numeric locationScore (0–10) to each lead based on the company's country — prioritizes high-value markets like US, UK, Canada, etc.

How it works:
• Pulls the country from the incoming item (from previous normalization step)
• Normalizes to lowercase + trims whitespace
• Matches against tiered lists of countries/regions
• Assigns score:
  - 10: US
  - 8: Tier 2 (UK, Canada, Australia, NZ)
  - 7: Western Europe, Nordics, East Asia, Singapore/HK
  - 6: Middle East (UAE, Israel, etc.)
  - 4: Other EU
  - 3: LatAm
  - 2: South Asia, SE Asia, Eastern Europe
  - 1: Africa
  - 0: Rest of world / unknown

Output fields added:
- normalizedCountry: original country value (for reference)
- locationScore: integer 0–10

Why this structure?
- Avoids node-name lookups (prevents execution hangs in recent n8n versions)
- Uses direct input data access ($input.all() + item.json)
- Safe fallbacks for missing/misnamed fields

Debug tips:
- If score is always 0: check upstream node outputs the country correctly (look at JSON preview)
- To change scoring: edit the arrays or if/else conditions
- Test with 1 item first (switch node to "Execute Once")

This keeps the workflow fast and reliable even with hundreds of leads.

nodes.name: Score Staff Count
nodes.notes:
Headcount Scoring (Templatized)
───────────────────────────────
Purpose: Scores companies by employee count (headcountScore 0–10) based on customizable ranges.

Customization (edit in code):
• sourceNodeName / sourceFieldPath → change if upstream node/field renamed
• defaultScore → fallback when count missing/invalid
• scoreRanges array → add/remove/edit buckets & scores
  Example: { min: 51, max: 200, score: 10 } = sweet spot

Current ranges:
- 1–10     → 3
- 11–50    → 7
- 51–200   → 10 (ideal target)
- 201–500  → 9
- 501–5000 → 8
- 5001+    → 7
- Missing  → 0

Output fields:
- staffCount: cleaned number
- headcountScore: 0–10

Tips:
- Test changes with small data first
- Use Infinity for "and above" ranges
- Numbers only — non-numeric input → 0

nodes.name: Sanitize Description
nodes.notes:
Sanitize Description
───────────────────
Purpose:
Cleans up the raw LinkedIn company description from the "LinkedIn Agent" node so it's Slack-friendly, readable, and short.

What it does:
• Safely grabs the description (handles missing data gracefully)
• Replaces fancy/smart quotes with normal ones
• Converts dashes/ellipses/newlines into clean single spaces
• Collapses extra whitespace
• Trims leading/trailing spaces
• Caps length at ~280 chars (optional, good for messaging)

Why we need this:
Raw LinkedIn descriptions often contain weird Unicode characters, multiple newlines, and promotional fluff that looks terrible in Slack notifications or short-form outputs.

Tips for debugging:
- If the node hangs: double-check the upstream node is exactly named "LinkedIn Agent" (no extra spaces).
- Test with a minimal version first: return items with hardcoded "TEST OK" to confirm execution.
- Output includes original data + new field: sanitized_description

Expected output field: sanitized_description (string)

nodes.name: Industry Scoring
nodes.notes:
Industry Normalization & Scoring (Templatized)
──────────────────────────────────────────────
Purpose: Converts raw industry strings → clean category + numeric score (higher = better fit).

Customization (edit in code):
• sourceFieldPath → change if industry is nested (e.g. "data.industry")
• fallbackCategory → default when no match
• industryCategories array:
  - Add/remove categories
  - Change score (0–10 recommended)
  - Edit keywords (partial, case-insensitive matches)
  - Order matters: higher-priority categories first

Current top priorities:
- Technology/Media → 10
- Financial/Fintech → 9
- Most others → 6 or lower

Output fields added:
- normalizedIndustry: clean category name
- originalIndustry: raw input for reference
- industryScore: numeric score

Tips:
- Test with varied industries first
- Use broad keywords to catch variations
- If too many "Other", add more specific keywords
- First match wins — reorder array for priority

Keeps workflow flexible for different lead criteria!

nodes.name: Algo Score
nodes.notes:
Final Lead Scoring (Templatized)
────────────────────────────────
Purpose: Combines 3 sub-scores (each 0–10) → final 0–100 score + rating label.

Customization (edit config object in code):
• sources → update node/field names if upstream changes
• weights → adjust relative importance (e.g. {headcount: 1.5, location: 1, industry: 2})
• ratingTiers → change thresholds/labels (highest first)
• defaultSubScore → fallback when data missing

Current setup:
- Equal weights (1:1:1)
- Max per sub-score = 10
- Ratings: ≥80 Excellent, ≥60 Good, ≥40 Moderate, else Poor

Output structure:
output.scores: {
  headcount, location, industry,
  totalRaw, finalScore (0–100), rating
}

Tips:
- Test with known good/bad leads
- To emphasize one factor → increase its weight
- Add new sub-scores by extending config.sources & weights

values.content:
You are an AI assistant that extracts names from email addresses. You receive an email address and should look at the part before the “@” to determine if it is a person’s first name or first and last name.
	1.	If you are extremely confident it is a real first name (e.g., “john”), output the first name only (e.g., “John”).
	2.	If you are extremely confident it is a real first and last name (e.g., “john.smith”), output both (e.g., “John Smith”).
	3.	If there is any doubt—meaning it looks like a role, department, organization, or anything that is not clearly a person’s name (e.g., “support,” “admin,” “info,” etc.)—output the word "there" as the Name and First Name without the quotes.

Capitalize names properly. Output only the name or there, with no additional text or punctuation.

Output the following:
- Name
- First Name
- Last Name

nodes.name: Extract LinkedIn Url
nodes.notes: This node will extract the LI profile URL

nodes.name: Check if Company or Personal LI Profile
nodes.notes: This node only passes through company LI profiles (and not personal LI profiles). If personal LI profiles still fits your ICP, route that via the 'False' branch.

nodes.name: Check root URL
nodes.notes: This node checks the status code to ensure the domain is healthy and responsive (so it does not waste any credits/scraping efforts).

nodes.name: Blacklist Regex Domains
nodes.notes: This removes trash emails, personal emails, employee emails and only passes through good B2B emails.

nodes.name: Check LinkedIn is not null
nodes.notes: Checks to make sure the LinkedIn profile is not empty/null.

nodes.name: Audit LI Results
nodes.notes:
This node audits the LI output from the agent to ensure there is a quality LI company profile (to continue the workflow). 

This implies that any company that has a complete LI profile is likely worth it whereas if they do not have a complete profile they are likely not a good fit.

nodes.name: Firecrawl Scrape
nodes.notes: This uses Firecrawl to scrape the website and extract LinkedIn URL. Typically websites will have their social profiles on their website (namely the footer) and this will extract the LinkedIn. Can also be used to extract other channels (such as YouTube, 𝕏, etc.).

format.prompt: Find the LinkedIn company profile URL for this organization. Look everywhere on the page, including the footer, header, about us section, and contact us page. Return only the complete LinkedIn URL.

nodes.name: website_tool
nodes.notes: This enriches company LI data.

parameters.toolDescription: Use this tool to get LinkedIn company information

parameters.content:
## Email handling
### This extracts the root domain and omits personal/trash emails

## Check if website exists and scrape
### This does a HEAD request to check if successful. If successful then scrapes

## Extract company LinkedIn URL & enrich company info
This extracts the LI URL from the footer of the site, omits personal LI profiles and enriches with followers, headcount, HQ location, description.
Uses scrapin.io as an enrichment tool for the AI Agent.

## Sanitize data for Slack, run scoring algo checks for qual and quant scores (based on ICP).
This ensures that data can be sent to Slack for notification and scores the country, headcount, industry, for a total qual/quant score.

nodes.notes: Sends to new-trials channel

parameters.content:
## Segment scores, verify lead is not already in Instantly, then add to campaign
### Breaks out into scoring ranges (Very High, High, Mid, Low. Then adds them to campaigns

# n8n Lead Qualification & Sales Intelligence Workflow (Top of Funnel)

### Overview / Purpose
This workflow automatically processes new free-trial / lead sign-ups in real time:

- Catches a webhook from any source (Webflow form, Intercom, custom agent, etc.)
- Filters out personal / disposable / .edu emails → only business emails continue
- Validates the company website is live
- Scrapes the website with Firecrawl to find the official LinkedIn company page
- Enriches the company via AI Agent (Scrapin.io) → pulls name, follower count, headcount, HQ location, industry, and clean description
- Runs a multi-factor scoring engine (Location + Headcount + Industry)
- Sends a beautiful rich Slack notification to the sales team with score, rating, and quick-action buttons
- Segments the lead by score tier (Very High / High / Mid / Low)
- Checks if the lead already exists in Instantly
- Adds qualified leads to the correct Instantly campaign with first/last name extracted from email

### Core Flow (Step-by-Step)

1. **Webhook** – Trigger (POST /new-lead)
2. **Extract Email Root Domain** – Pulls `@company.com` part
3. **Blacklist Regex Domains** – Blocks all personal, free, and .edu emails
4. **Check Email Not Null** – Safety gate
5. **Check if Website Exists** – HEAD request (continues only on 200-399)
6. **Firecrawl Scrape** – Scrapes homepage looking for LinkedIn URL in footer/links
7. **Extract LinkedIn Url** – Code node that finds and cleans `/company/` URL
8. **Check LinkedIn Not Null + Is Company Page** – Rejects personal profiles
9. **LinkedIn Agent** (AI Agent)
   - Uses Scrapin.io enrichment tool
   - Structured output: company_name, followers, employee_count, headquarters_location, industry, description
10. **Audit LI Results** – Ensures all key fields returned
11. **Sanitize Description** – Cleans Unicode, newlines, caps length for Slack
12. **Normalize Country** – GPT-4.1-nano turns "Sheridan, US" → "United States"
13. **Score Country** – Code node (0–10 points based on market tier)
14. **Score Staff Count** – Code node (0–10 points based on headcount buckets)
15. **Industry Scoring** – Code node (normalizes raw industry + 0–10 points)
16. **Algo Score** – Final score = weighted average → 0–100 + rating label ("Excellent Fit", "Good Fit", etc.)
17. **Send to Slack** – Rich blocks with:
    - Email, Company Name, Location (+score), Staff Count (+score), Followers, Industry (+score)
    - Clean description
    - Final Score + Rating
    - Buttons: Visit LinkedIn + Visit Website
18. **Score-Based Routing**
    - Very High (90–100)
    - High (70–89)
    - Mid (50–69)
    - Low (0–49)
19. **Instantly Checks** – For each tier: search if lead already exists
20. **Extract Name From Email** (GPT) – Turns `john.smith@company.com` → First/Last Name (or "there")
21. **Wait** nodes (optional delay before adding)
22. **Add Lead to Campaign** – Pushes to Instantly with website custom field

### Scoring Logic (Fully Configurable in Code Nodes)

Based on your ICP, narrow down your scoring based on how you want to segment.

### What Makes This Workflow Powerful
- Zero manual work after webhook
- Rich, actionable Slack alerts (sales team loves it)
- Smart scoring that matches real ICP
- Built-in deduplication with Instantly
- Fully modular code nodes (easy to tweak scoring without breaking anything)
- Heavy error handling and safety gates

---

**This is a complete, production-ready, public template.**  
You can import the JSON and it will run immediately after you connect your own:
- Webhook source
- Firecrawl API
- Scrapin.io (or alternative)
- OpenAI
- Slack
- Instantly

Any questions/comments/concerns, please email brandon@topoffunnel.com
Built by Brandon Charleson
https://www.youtube.com/@brandoncharleson
https://www.linkedin.com/in/brandon-charleson 
https://x.com/brandon_ai

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
